channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid Museum & Archives Opportunity for International Students
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden has officially opened applications for its highly sought-after Exhibition History Cataloging Internship 2026 in Washington, DC. This exciting paid internship offers undergraduate students, graduate students, and recent graduates the opportunity to gain hands-on museum experience at one of the world’s leading modern and contemporary art museums. Hosted […]

The post Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid Museum & Archives Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden has officially opened applications for its highly sought-after Exhibition History Cataloging Internship 2026 in Washington, DC. This exciting paid internship offers undergraduate students, graduate students, and recent graduates the opportunity to gain hands-on museum experience at one of the world’s leading modern and contemporary art museums.

Hosted by the Documentation and Archives Department within the Collection Care and Management division, the internship provides practical exposure to archival research, museum cataloging systems, collection management, and historical exhibition documentation. Participants will work directly with museum records and databases while developing professional research and archival skills in a globally respected institution.

Applications are open until July 24, 2026.

About the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026

The Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden is part of the Smithsonian Institution and is internationally recognized for its significant collection of modern and contemporary art. Through this internship, selected candidates will contribute to the preservation and organization of historical exhibition records while learning museum documentation procedures used by professional archivists and collection managers.

The internship specifically focuses on exhibition history cataloging and database management using TMS (The Museum System), a professional museum collections database widely used across museums and cultural institutions.

This internship is ideal for students and emerging professionals interested in:

  • Museum studies
  • Art history
  • Archives and records management
  • Collection documentation
  • Research and cataloging
  • Cultural heritage preservation

Internship Responsibilities

Selected interns will support the Documentation and Archives Department with several important museum tasks, including:

1. Exhibition Record Cataloging

Interns will add historic exhibition records and exhibition checklists into the museum’s cataloging database system.

2. Database Documentation

Participants will learn how museums organize and maintain historical records using professional cataloging software and archival standards.

3. Research and Verification

Interns will review exhibition paper records, publications, and archival materials to ensure historical accuracy before entering data into the museum database.

4. Checklist Creation and Cross-Referencing

The internship includes training in:

  • Checklist development
  • Cross-reference techniques
  • Metadata organization
  • Historical documentation methods

5. Archival and Museum Practice Exposure

Interns will gain first-hand understanding of:

  • Museum archival procedures
  • Collection care operations
  • Documentation standards
  • Professional museum workflows

Internship Benefits

The Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship offers several valuable academic and professional benefits for participants.

Financial Support

  • Selected interns will receive a stipend of $3,200.

Professional Experience

Participants will gain hands-on experience working in a Smithsonian museum environment and build practical skills in:

  • Museum cataloging
  • Archival research
  • Documentation systems
  • Art history research
  • Database management

Career Development

This internship can strengthen future applications for:

  • Museum careers
  • Graduate school programs
  • Art history programs
  • Archival studies
  • Library and information science careers
  • Cultural heritage institutions

Networking Opportunities

Interns will work closely with museum professionals and gain insight into the operations of one of the world’s most respected museum institutions.

Internship Duration and Schedule

  • Internship Start Date: September 28, 2026
  • Internship End Date: November 20, 2026
  • Duration: 8 weeks
  • Weekly Commitment: 16 hours per week
  • Work Format: On-site
  • Location: Washington, DC

Work schedules and internship hours will be arranged mutually between the selected intern and the internship supervisor before the program begins.

Who Can Apply?

The internship is open to:

  • Currently enrolled undergraduate students
  • Graduate students
  • Doctoral students
  • Recent graduates who completed their degree within one year prior to the internship start date
  • Students graduating during the internship period

International applicants are encouraged to apply, and there is no citizenship requirement.

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Demonstrated academic interest in museums, archives, or collection management
  • Career goals aligned with museum and archival work
  • Interest in art history research
  • Attention to detail and research capability

Required Application Materials

Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents through the Smithsonian Online Academic Appointment System (SOLAA):

  1. Online application form
  2. Two letters of reference
  3. Academic transcripts (unofficial transcripts accepted)
  4. Personal essay addressing:
    • Why you are interested in this internship
    • How the opportunity will advance your academic or professional goals

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: July 24, 2026
  • Decision Notifications: August 3–7, 2026
  • Internship Start Date: September 28, 2026
  • Internship End Date: November 20, 2026

Why This Internship Matters

The Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship is more than a short-term placement. It is an opportunity to gain practical museum experience within one of the world’s most respected cultural institutions.

For students interested in museum careers, archives, cultural preservation, or art history, this internship provides direct exposure to professional museum documentation practices while helping participants develop valuable research, cataloging, and archival skills.

Because the program welcomes international applicants and offers a stipend, it is also an excellent opportunity for students worldwide seeking international museum experience in the United States.

How to Apply for the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship 2026

To apply, candidates must complete their application through SOLAA.

Application Steps

  1. Create an account or log in to SOLAA
  2. Click the “Start Application” button
  3. Search for the program
  4. Select:
    “Hirshhorn Exhibition History Cataloging Internship”
  5. Click “Apply Now”
  6. Upload all required documents and submit the application before the deadline

Learn More & Apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid Museum & Archives Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 | Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities in the UK
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications Open for Two Prestigious British Library Research Fellowships The British Library has officially launched applications for the highly anticipated Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027, offering two funded fellowship opportunities for postdoctoral early-career researchers who are passionate about cultural heritage, archives, artificial intelligence, digital discovery, and historical collections. With applications closing on 15 […]

The post The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 | Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for Two Prestigious British Library Research Fellowships

The British Library has officially launched applications for the highly anticipated Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027, offering two funded fellowship opportunities for postdoctoral early-career researchers who are passionate about cultural heritage, archives, artificial intelligence, digital discovery, and historical collections.

With applications closing on 15 July 2026, this fellowship programme provides researchers with a unique opportunity to work directly with one of the world’s most important research libraries while collaborating with expert curators, archivists, and specialist research teams.

The 12-month fellowship programme is designed to bridge the gap between doctoral study and long-term professional research careers by equipping fellows with advanced practical, interdisciplinary, and future-focused skills within the cultural and heritage sector.

The fellowship will officially begin from 1 September 2026, with part-time arrangements potentially extending the fellowship duration.

About the Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme

The Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme was established through a generous donation from Joanna and Graham Barker to support postdoctoral researchers at a critical stage in their academic and professional journey.

The initiative focuses on helping early-career researchers gain:

  • Practical institutional experience
  • Research leadership skills
  • Project management exposure
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities
  • Cultural heritage sector expertise
  • Professional mentoring and development
  • Access to world-class archival collections

The British Library aims to create a structured pathway for emerging researchers to thrive within the rapidly evolving knowledge economy and research landscape.

This fellowship particularly addresses the significant funding gap faced by postdoctoral researchers across academia and the cultural sector.

Fellowship Research Themes for 2026

Applicants are invited to co-design a research project with the British Library based on one of the following two specialist themes.

1. Colonial Legal Deposit and the British Library Newspaper Collection

This fellowship is based within the:

  • News & Moving Image Department
  • Sound & Vision Teams

Research Focus

The fellowship explores the historical and contemporary implications of colonial legal deposit systems and their relationship with newspaper collections preserved by the British Library.

Potential areas of exploration may include:

  • Colonial-era newspaper acquisition systems
  • Historical legal deposit practices
  • Representation and preservation of colonial narratives
  • Media archives and empire studies
  • Accessibility of historical newspaper collections
  • Decolonisation of archival practices
  • Historical metadata and discoverability
  • Newspaper preservation and interpretation

Researchers interested in:

  • Media history
  • Colonial studies
  • Digital archives
  • Historical collections
  • Cultural memory
  • Information preservation

may find this theme especially relevant.

2. Local Large Language Models for Personal Digital Archives

This fellowship is based within:

  • Contemporary British and Irish Archives and Manuscripts

Research Focus

The second fellowship investigates how Local Large Language Models (LLMs) can improve the discovery, accessibility, interpretation, and management of personal digital archives.

The project aligns with the growing importance of artificial intelligence in archives and cultural heritage institutions.

Possible research areas include:

  • Ethical AI implementation in archives
  • Local LLM development
  • AI-powered archival discovery tools
  • Digital preservation
  • Metadata enhancement
  • Privacy-aware AI systems
  • Personal digital archive accessibility
  • Human-centered archival technologies
  • Machine learning for cultural heritage collections

This opportunity is ideal for researchers working in:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Digital Humanities
  • Archival Science
  • Computational Humanities
  • Library and Information Science
  • Machine Learning
  • Cultural Informatics
  • Digital Preservation

Fellowship Benefits and Opportunities

Selected fellows will receive an extensive range of professional and research benefits throughout the programme.

Key Fellowship Advantages

1. Fully Funded Research Opportunity

Participants will undertake a funded 12-month fellowship embedded within the British Library.

2. Collaboration with Leading Experts

Fellows will work closely with:

  • Curators
  • Archivists
  • Research specialists
  • Digital humanities professionals
  • Cultural heritage experts

3. Access to World-Class Collections

Researchers will gain privileged access to:

  • British Library archival collections
  • Newspaper repositories
  • Manuscripts
  • Digital archives
  • Cultural heritage datasets

4. Professional Development

The fellowship includes:

  • Research training
  • Project management experience
  • Leadership development
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Institutional research exposure

5. Career Advancement

The scheme is specifically designed to strengthen the long-term career prospects of postdoctoral researchers across academia and the GLAM sector.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The British Library highlights that postdoctoral researchers often face limited funding opportunities after completing their PhDs.

Research studies show that career precarity disproportionately affects:

  • Women researchers
  • Researchers of colour
  • Researchers with disabilities
  • First-generation academics
  • Researchers with caring responsibilities

This fellowship directly addresses those challenges by creating inclusive and sustainable research pathways.

Main Objectives of the Fellowship Scheme

The programme aims to:

  1. Build upon the success of collaborative doctoral programmes
  2. Support the transition toward independent research careers
  3. Expand opportunities in the cultural and heritage sector
  4. Strengthen interdisciplinary research practices
  5. Enhance discoverability and interpretation of collections
  6. Promote equality, diversity, and inclusion in research
  7. Generate impactful institutional knowledge
  8. Improve public engagement with cultural collections

Fellowship Duration

  • Duration: 12 months
  • Start Date: 1 September 2026
  • Flexible part-time arrangements may extend the duration

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme, applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Mandatory Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a doctorate in a relevant subject
    OR
  • Possess equivalent professional experience and skills

Applicants must also:

  • Demonstrate commitment to working with the cultural and heritage sector
  • Be based in the UK
  • Be eligible to work in the UK for the full fellowship duration
  • Self-identify as an early-career postdoctoral researcher according to AHRC guidance

Important PhD Completion Requirement

Applicants who have:

  • Passed their viva with minor corrections before the deadline are eligible

However:

  • Applicants requiring major corrections that extend beyond the application deadline are not eligible.

Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Commitment

The British Library strongly promotes inclusive talent development and equitable research opportunities across:

  • Galleries
  • Libraries
  • Archives
  • Museums (GLAM sector)

The fellowship prioritises accessibility, diversity, and broad participation in cultural heritage research.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 July 2026

Interested applicants should prepare their proposals early and carefully review the research themes before applying.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is highly suitable for:

  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Digital humanities scholars
  • Archivists
  • AI and machine learning researchers
  • Library and information science professionals
  • Cultural heritage practitioners
  • Humanities researchers
  • Computational humanities specialists
  • Media historians
  • Archive technologists

Final Thoughts

The Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers seeking to combine academic excellence with real-world cultural heritage impact.

By embedding fellows within the British Library’s expert teams and collections, the programme creates a rare environment where cutting-edge research, historical archives, artificial intelligence, and interdisciplinary collaboration intersect.

For researchers aiming to build a meaningful career in academia, digital humanities, archival innovation, or cultural heritage institutions, this fellowship provides both the platform and professional support needed to advance their work on an international level.

Learn More & Apply

Applicants must submit a research proposal aligned with one of the two fellowship themes.

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 | Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women-Led Business Grant 2026: Apply for Up to $750,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women Entrepreneurs Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Women entrepreneurs around the world now have an outstanding opportunity to secure catalytic funding to scale their businesses through the Women-Led Business Grant 2026. The program is currently accepting applications from women-led and women-owned businesses seeking financial support to expand their impact, strengthen operations, and accelerate sustainable growth. The grant provides non-dilutive funding ranging from […]

The post Women-Led Business Grant 2026: Apply for Up to $750,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women Entrepreneurs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women entrepreneurs around the world now have an outstanding opportunity to secure catalytic funding to scale their businesses through the Women-Led Business Grant 2026. The program is currently accepting applications from women-led and women-owned businesses seeking financial support to expand their impact, strengthen operations, and accelerate sustainable growth.

The grant provides non-dilutive funding ranging from $25,000 to $750,000, allowing entrepreneurs to grow their ventures without giving up ownership or equity. In addition to funding, selected businesses will receive leadership coaching and access to a global peer network designed to support long-term business success.

If you are a woman entrepreneur leading an innovative business with growth potential, this funding opportunity could help you expand into new markets, improve operational capacity, invest in technology, and create lasting economic impact.

About the Women-Led Business Grant

The Women-Led Business Grant is designed to support businesses that are founded, owned, or led by women. The initiative recognizes the important role women entrepreneurs play in driving innovation, creating employment opportunities, strengthening economies, and promoting inclusive growth across industries.

Beyond financial support, the program offers valuable resources that help businesses become more competitive and sustainable. Successful applicants benefit from:

  • Non-dilutive grant funding
  • Leadership coaching
  • Access to a global network of entrepreneurs
  • Opportunities to accelerate business growth
  • Support for businesses across all sectors

The program is open to eligible businesses globally, making it an excellent opportunity for women entrepreneurs from both developed and emerging markets.

Funding Amount

Successful applicants may receive funding between:

  • Minimum Grant: $25,000
  • Maximum Grant: $750,000

The amount awarded will depend on the project’s scope, business needs, and overall impact potential.

What Can the Grant Fund?

The Women-Led Business Grant supports a wide variety of business activities that contribute to growth, innovation, and sustainability.

Funding may be used for:

1. Working Capital

Businesses can strengthen day-to-day operations by covering operational expenses and maintaining healthy cash flow.

2. Pilot Deployments

Entrepreneurs can test new products, services, technologies, or business models before scaling them.

3. Technical Assistance and Capacity Building

Grant funding can be invested in strengthening organizational systems, improving management practices, and building staff capacity.

4. Climate and ESG-Aligned Investments

Businesses working on environmentally sustainable or socially responsible initiatives can use the funding to advance climate resilience and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) goals.

5. Market Research and Feasibility Studies

Applicants may conduct market assessments, customer research, and feasibility analyses before entering new markets or launching new products.

6. Equipment and Technology Procurement

Businesses can purchase machinery, digital tools, software, equipment, and technology needed to improve productivity and competitiveness.

7. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning

Funding may also support systems that measure business performance, project outcomes, and organizational impact for continuous improvement.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, businesses must meet at least one of the following requirements:

  • Be at least 51% owned by women, or
  • Have women serving in C-suite leadership positions.

These eligibility requirements ensure that the funding directly supports women-led enterprises and promotes female leadership in business.

Why You Should Apply

The Women-Led Business Grant offers much more than financial assistance.

Key benefits include:

  • Access to grant funding without giving up company ownership.
  • Leadership coaching from experienced professionals.
  • Global networking opportunities with fellow entrepreneurs.
  • Support for businesses operating in any industry.
  • Funding for innovation, technology, sustainability, and business expansion.
  • Opportunity to strengthen long-term organizational capacity.
  • Increased visibility and credibility for supported businesses.

Application Process

Applying for the Women-Led Business Grant is designed to be straightforward.

The application process includes:

  1. Complete the online application.
  2. Submit the required business information.
  3. Describe how the requested funding will be used.
  4. Submit your application for review.

The application is estimated to take approximately 15 minutes to complete.

Applicants can expect funding decisions within 4 to 6 weeks after submission.

Application Fee

There are no application fees required to apply for this grant.

Program Standards

The grant program operates according to World Bank Group standards, ensuring a structured and transparent funding process.

Application Deadline

Deadline: August 20, 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues.

Grant Summary

Item Details
Grant Name Women-Led Business Grant
Funding $25,000–$750,000
Region Global
Status Open
Application Fee None
Application Time Approximately 15 minutes
Decision Timeline 4–6 weeks
Deadline August 20, 2026

Apply

Click here to apply

Final Thoughts

Access to growth capital remains one of the greatest challenges facing women entrepreneurs worldwide. The Women-Led Business Grant addresses this challenge by providing significant non-dilutive funding alongside leadership development and international networking opportunities.

Whether your business is looking to expand operations, invest in technology, conduct research, improve sustainability, or strengthen organizational capacity, this grant offers a valuable opportunity to accelerate growth while maintaining ownership of your company.

Eligible women-led businesses are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit before the August 20, 2026 deadline.

For more information, visit  the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Women-Led Business Grant 2026: Apply for Up to $750,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women Entrepreneurs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026: Research Gender-Based Violence in the Workplace
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The University of Wollongong (UOW) has announced an exciting PhD scholarship opportunity for researchers passionate about addressing one of the most critical social and workplace issues of our time — gender-based violence in the context of work. This prestigious research opportunity invites motivated scholars to contribute to groundbreaking academic work exploring how gender-based violence impacts […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026: Research Gender-Based Violence in the Workplace appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Wollongong (UOW) has announced an exciting PhD scholarship opportunity for researchers passionate about addressing one of the most critical social and workplace issues of our time — gender-based violence in the context of work.

This prestigious research opportunity invites motivated scholars to contribute to groundbreaking academic work exploring how gender-based violence impacts workplaces, employment systems, professional environments, and worker wellbeing across different industries and communities.

Applications and research proposals are due on June 29, 2026.

About the PhD Scholarship

This PhD scholarship at UOW is designed for emerging researchers who are committed to producing impactful, socially relevant, and policy-oriented research. The project focuses specifically on gender-based violence within workplace and employment contexts, an issue increasingly recognized globally as a major human rights, social justice, and labor concern.

The scholarship presents an opportunity for candidates to work closely with academic experts while contributing to research that can influence workplace policies, organizational cultures, legal frameworks, and support systems for workers affected by violence and discrimination.

Researchers selected for this program will have the opportunity to:

  • Conduct high-level academic research
  • Explore workplace-related gender inequalities and violence
  • Develop evidence-based recommendations
  • Contribute to policy discussions and reform
  • Engage with interdisciplinary perspectives
  • Build expertise in a globally relevant research area

Research Focus Areas

Applicants may explore a wide range of themes related to gender-based violence and work. Potential research areas may include:

  1. Workplace harassment and discrimination
  2. Gendered violence in professional settings
  3. Power dynamics and labor exploitation
  4. Organizational responses to workplace violence
  5. Domestic violence and its impact on employment
  6. Gender inequality in labor systems
  7. Psychological and emotional impacts on workers
  8. Legal protections and workplace policies
  9. Prevention and intervention frameworks
  10. Vulnerable workers and marginalized communities
  11. Intersectionality in workplace violence experiences
  12. Global and comparative workplace studies

Candidates are encouraged to develop innovative and impactful proposals aligned with these broader themes.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Gender-based violence continues to affect millions of individuals worldwide, including within workplaces and employment systems. Research in this field has become increasingly important for governments, institutions, employers, and advocacy organizations seeking to create safer and more equitable professional environments.

This PhD scholarship offers scholars the chance to contribute meaningful research that can:

  • Influence workplace policies
  • Support social justice initiatives
  • Improve worker protections
  • Inform government and institutional reforms
  • Enhance organizational accountability
  • Promote inclusive and safe work cultures

The research outcomes from this program could have long-term academic, professional, and societal impact.

Benefits of the Scholarship

The University of Wollongong provides an excellent academic and research environment for doctoral researchers. Although applicants should review the official scholarship advertisement for exact funding details and eligibility requirements, benefits may include:

  • Tuition fee support
  • Research funding assistance
  • Academic supervision and mentorship
  • Access to university research facilities
  • Networking and collaboration opportunities
  • Professional development support
  • Opportunities to publish and present research internationally

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official scholarship advertisement carefully for precise funding information and conditions.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Graduates seeking to pursue doctoral research
  • Researchers passionate about gender justice and workplace equity
  • Individuals interested in social policy and labor issues
  • Academics aiming to influence real-world workplace reform
  • Professionals with backgrounds in sociology, law, psychology, gender studies, public policy, labor relations, or related fields

Strong research, analytical, and proposal-writing skills will be highly valuable for applicants.

Application Process

Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare a strong and well-structured research proposal that clearly demonstrates:

  • The relevance of the research topic
  • Proposed research objectives
  • Methodology and theoretical framework
  • Potential contribution to knowledge and policy
  • Alignment with the scholarship theme

Applicants should carefully follow all submission instructions provided in the official scholarship advertisement.

Important Deadline

Application and proposal submission deadline:

June 29, 2026

Applicants are advised to begin preparing their research proposals as early as possible to ensure a competitive submission.

 

Final Thoughts

The UOW PhD Scholarship on gender-based violence in the context of work represents an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers to engage in meaningful and impactful scholarship.

As workplace safety, equity, and inclusion continue to gain global attention, research in this field has the potential to shape policies, improve worker wellbeing, and drive lasting institutional change.

Candidates passionate about social justice, workplace reform, and gender equity are strongly encouraged to apply and contribute to this important area of research.

For further information, applicants should review the official scholarship advertisement and contact the relevant coordinators with any questions regarding the opportunity.

Learn More & Apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026: Research Gender-Based Violence in the Workplace appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America & the Caribbean
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the prestigious Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) 2027 at Georgetown University in Washington, D.C. This highly competitive and transformative leadership development program is designed for emerging leaders across Latin America and the Caribbean who are already creating meaningful change within their communities and organizations. Through a powerful combination of […]

The post Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America & the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the prestigious Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) 2027 at Georgetown University in Washington, D.C.

This highly competitive and transformative leadership development program is designed for emerging leaders across Latin America and the Caribbean who are already creating meaningful change within their communities and organizations. Through a powerful combination of virtual learning, mentorship, leadership development, and in-person academic engagement at Georgetown University, participants will gain the tools, networks, and strategic vision needed to expand their social impact across the region.

The Global Competitiveness Leadership (GCL) Program is hosted by Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program (LALP) and is recognized as one of the region’s most influential leadership experiences for young changemakers.

The 2027 cohort will run from January 11 to March 19, 2027, combining online sessions with an intensive in-person experience in Washington, D.C.

Application Deadline

Applications for the GCL 2027 Program are open from:

  • Opening Date: June 15, 2026
  • Deadline: August 14, 2026 (11:59 PM EDT)

Selected applicants will receive admission notifications by November 2, 2026.

About the Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL)

The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program is an academic and experiential leadership initiative focused on developing the next generation of influential leaders capable of promoting competitiveness, innovation, sustainable development, and social transformation throughout Latin America and the Caribbean.

The program supports leaders who are already working on projects with long-term social impact and seeks to amplify their effectiveness through advanced leadership training, collaboration, and regional networking opportunities.

Participants become part of a strong network of changemakers, innovators, and professionals committed to driving positive transformation within their countries and communities.

Program Structure and Duration

The GCL 2027 Program is a 10-week hybrid experience that combines virtual engagement with immersive in-person learning at Georgetown University.

Program Dates

January 11 – March 19, 2027

Virtual Component

January 11 – February 12, 2027

Participants will complete four weeks of virtual programming, generally scheduled on:

  • Mondays
  • Wednesdays
  • Selected Fridays

Sessions are typically conducted between 9:00 AM and 1:15 PM EST.

The virtual component also includes one asynchronous week that allows participants to prepare for travel and continue developing their projects with mentorship support.

In-Person Component in Washington, D.C.

February 16 – March 19, 2027

Participants will travel to Washington, D.C. for five weeks of intensive in-person programming at Georgetown University.

The in-person sessions are conducted:

  • Monday through Friday
  • 9:30 AM – 4:15 PM EST

The on-campus experience includes leadership workshops, collaborative projects, mentoring opportunities, academic sessions, networking events, and strategic discussions with regional leaders and experts.

Scholarship and Financial Support

The total value of the GCL Program is approximately USD $25,000.

To make the program more accessible, Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program offers scholarships to admitted participants.

During the application process, candidates can request financial assistance by:

  • Indicating that they wish to apply for a scholarship
  • Specifying the scholarship amount requested
  • Explaining their financial need

Important Scholarship Information

Even participants receiving the maximum scholarship award will still be required to contribute USD $3,500 toward participation costs.

What the Program Fee Covers

The program fee includes:

  • Ten weeks of virtual and in-person instruction
  • Academic materials and learning resources
  • Continuous mentorship and support throughout the program
  • Shared accommodation during the Washington, D.C. residency
  • Local transportation for official program activities
  • Basic emergency medical insurance during the in-person component
  • Official certificate of participation from Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program

Expenses Not Covered

Participants are responsible for covering:

  • International airfare
  • Meals
  • Personal expenses
  • Incidentals
  • Any costs not specifically included in the program fee

Accommodation Information

All admitted participants are required to stay in the accommodation arranged by the program.

Housing is provided in shared apartments, usually with:

  • Two participants per room
  • Up to four participants per apartment

Please note that family members or guests are not permitted in the program housing.

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the GCL 2027 Program, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 26 and 35 years old by the application deadline.

Nationality and Residency

Eligible applicants must be citizens and residents of approved countries in Latin America and the Caribbean.

Applicants from Spain and Portugal may also apply if their projects positively impact the Latin American region.

Educational Qualification

Candidates must hold a university degree from an accredited institution and demonstrate a strong academic record.

English Language Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate excellent English proficiency in:

  • Reading
  • Writing
  • Speaking

Professional or Volunteer Experience

Applicants must have at least one year of professional or volunteer experience related to social impact, leadership, or community development initiatives.

Leadership and Impact Requirement

Candidates must currently be leading a project that demonstrates positive, sustainable, and long-term impact within their community or region.

Commitment to Community Development

Participants must commit to returning to their countries or communities after the program to apply the knowledge and skills gained through GCL.

Applicants will be required to sign a formal agreement confirming this commitment.

Failure to comply with this agreement may require reimbursement of scholarship funds received.

Why You Should Apply for GCL 2027

The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program offers much more than academic training. It provides participants with:

  • Advanced leadership development
  • Access to influential regional and global networks
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Exposure to international perspectives and policy discussions
  • Opportunities to strengthen social impact projects
  • Practical tools for sustainable development and innovation
  • Collaboration with high-performing changemakers from across the region

Participants leave the program with stronger leadership capacity, expanded regional influence, and a deeper understanding of how to create scalable impact.

Final Thoughts

The Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 is a life-changing opportunity for emerging leaders who are passionate about creating lasting social impact across Latin America and the Caribbean.

If you are already leading meaningful change in your community and want to strengthen your leadership skills, expand your regional influence, and connect with a powerful network of changemakers, this program could be the next major step in your journey.

With scholarships available and access to one of the world’s leading academic institutions, GCL 2027 offers a rare platform for visionary leaders committed to transforming their communities and shaping the future of the region.

Learn more and apply

Interested candidates must submit their application and all required supporting documents through the official Georgetown University application portal before the deadline.

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America & the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Women’s rights organizations, feminist movements, grassroots collectives, and gender justice activists across Africa now have access to an important funding opportunity through the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Grant Program. This initiative is designed to provide rapid-response and flexible funding to support activists, organizations, and movements responding to urgent threats, emergencies, advocacy opportunities, and critical moments […]

The post Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women’s rights organizations, feminist movements, grassroots collectives, and gender justice activists across Africa now have access to an important funding opportunity through the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Grant Program.

This initiative is designed to provide rapid-response and flexible funding to support activists, organizations, and movements responding to urgent threats, emergencies, advocacy opportunities, and critical moments affecting women’s rights and gender justice.

The grant program is managed by Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa), a pan-African feminist fund dedicated to supporting women, girls, and gender-diverse individuals working to promote human rights, equality, justice, and social transformation throughout the continent.

Unlike traditional funding programs that often involve long waiting periods, this initiative focuses on speed, flexibility, and urgent intervention, allowing activists and movements to respond quickly when immediate action is necessary.

About the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa)

Urgent Action Fund–Africa is a feminist and women’s human rights fund that supports courageous activists, grassroots organizations, and movements that are advancing gender justice and protecting vulnerable communities across Africa.

The organization prioritizes initiatives that:

  • Protect women human rights defenders
  • Support feminist movement-building
  • Strengthen grassroots organizing
  • Respond to emergencies and crises
  • Advance strategic advocacy opportunities
  • Promote safety, resilience, and wellbeing for activists

The fund recognizes that women’s rights defenders and gender justice activists often face threats, violence, political repression, discrimination, and emergency situations that require immediate support and intervention.

Through this rapid-response funding mechanism, UAF-Africa helps ensure that activists and organizations can act swiftly during critical moments.

What the UAF-Africa Grant Supports

The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program provides flexible financial support for urgent situations and opportunities related to women’s rights and gender justice.

Selected applicants may receive funding for activities such as:

1. Emergency Response Initiatives

Organizations and activists facing urgent threats or crises may receive support to address immediate safety and protection needs.

This may include:

  • Emergency relocation
  • Security measures
  • Crisis response activities
  • Protection of activists under threat
  • Legal or advocacy support during emergencies

2. Advocacy and Campaign Activities

Funding may support strategic advocacy efforts that advance women’s rights and gender justice.

Examples include:

  • Public awareness campaigns
  • Policy advocacy initiatives
  • Human rights mobilization efforts
  • Feminist advocacy campaigns
  • Community organizing activities

3. Movement-Building and Organizing

The fund supports initiatives that strengthen feminist movements and grassroots networks.

This may include:

  • Coalition-building activities
  • Leadership development
  • Community mobilization
  • Capacity strengthening
  • Networking and collaboration efforts

4. Protection of Women Human Rights Defenders

Women human rights defenders often face intimidation, violence, and threats while advocating for justice.

The fund prioritizes:

  • Safety and wellbeing initiatives
  • Trauma support
  • Activist protection measures
  • Security planning
  • Safe organizing spaces

5. Strategic Opportunities for Gender Justice

The program also supports time-sensitive opportunities that can create meaningful progress for women’s rights and equality.

These opportunities may involve:

  • Legislative advocacy windows
  • International advocacy opportunities
  • Community engagement moments
  • Rapid-response mobilization
  • Strategic feminist interventions

Benefits of the UAF-Africa Grant Program

Applicants selected for the program can benefit from several important forms of support, including:

  • Rapid-response funding for urgent situations
  • Flexible grants tailored to immediate needs
  • Support for advocacy and organizing
  • Assistance for activist protection and safety
  • Funding for feminist movement-building
  • Resources to strengthen resilience and wellbeing
  • Opportunity to respond quickly during critical moments
  • Support for grassroots and informal activist groups

One of the key strengths of the program is its flexibility, allowing recipients to use the funding in ways that best respond to their urgent realities and advocacy priorities.

Who Can Apply?

The opportunity is open to a wide range of applicants working on women’s rights and gender justice issues across Africa.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Women’s rights organizations
  • Feminist groups and networks
  • Grassroots collectives
  • Women human rights defenders
  • Gender-diverse activists
  • Community-based organizations
  • Informal activist groups and collectives
  • Women and girls-led initiatives
  • Social justice movements working on gender equality

Both registered and informal groups may be eligible, provided they are actively involved in feminist organizing, women’s rights advocacy, or gender justice work.

Priority Focus Areas

The program prioritizes initiatives working in areas such as:

Women’s Rights and Gender Equality

Projects focused on advancing equality, inclusion, dignity, and justice for women and marginalized communities.

Feminist Organizing and Movement-Building

Initiatives that strengthen feminist leadership, solidarity, collaboration, and grassroots mobilization.

Protection of Activists

Support for organizations and individuals facing threats, harassment, or security challenges while defending human rights.

Emergency Response and Crisis Intervention

Rapid interventions during emergencies, conflicts, political crises, or urgent human rights situations.

Advocacy and Campaigns

Strategic campaigns and policy advocacy efforts aimed at advancing gender justice and protecting rights.

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on several important factors.

These include:

  1. Urgency of the situation or opportunity
  2. Potential impact on women’s rights and gender justice
  3. Strength and relevance of the proposed response
  4. Safety and wellbeing considerations for activists
  5. Alignment with the mission of UAF-Africa
  6. Capacity to implement urgent activities effectively
  7. Contribution to feminist movement-building

The program places strong emphasis on timely action and meaningful impact.

Apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026.

Deadline

Deadline: Rolling

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, meaning eligible organizations and activists can apply whenever urgent situations or strategic opportunities arise.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible once an urgent need emerges.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across Africa, women human rights defenders and feminist activists continue to play a vital role in promoting justice, equality, and social transformation.

However, many activists operate under difficult conditions that include political pressure, insecurity, discrimination, and limited access to rapid funding.

The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program helps bridge this gap by providing fast, accessible, and flexible support exactly when it is most needed.

This funding opportunity empowers activists and organizations to:

  • Protect vulnerable communities
  • Strengthen feminist movements
  • Respond rapidly to emergencies
  • Advance gender justice initiatives
  • Build resilience and sustainability

For organizations and activists working tirelessly to defend human rights and promote equality across Africa, this program represents an important opportunity for timely and impactful support.

 

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs & Women with Disabilities in Uganda
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Open for the LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship Program Are you a young woman passionate about science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) and interested in becoming a teacher? The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship offers an incredible opportunity for eligible female students to pursue a Bachelor […]

The post LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs & Women with Disabilities in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship Program

Are you a young woman passionate about science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) and interested in becoming a teacher? The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship offers an incredible opportunity for eligible female students to pursue a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) at selected universities across Uganda with full financial support.

The scholarship is designed to increase female participation in STEM teaching, empower future women leaders in education, and address the shortage of qualified female science teachers in Uganda’s secondary schools.

About the Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program

The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program is a five-year initiative funded by the Mastercard Foundation and implemented through a consortium of ten education-focused organizations, including the Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF), UNICEF, British Council, VVOB, Edukans International, Brainwave Careers Uganda, STiR Education, PEAS Uganda, Teach for Uganda, and FENU.

Implemented under the leadership of Uganda’s Ministry of Education and Sports, the program supports the Mastercard Foundation’s Young Africa Works strategy, which aims to enable millions of young people across Africa to access dignified and fulfilling work by 2030.

Through its Recruit, Train, Lead, and Motivate pillars, the program seeks to improve the quality of teaching and learning in secondary schools while expanding opportunities for young women in STEM education.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive support throughout their studies, including:

Full Tuition and Functional Fees

The scholarship covers all tuition and university-related functional fees for the duration of the Bachelor of Science with Education program.

Accommodation Support

Recipients will receive accommodation support, ensuring a safe and conducive learning environment.

Free Laptop

Each scholar will receive a laptop to facilitate digital learning, research, and academic success.

Scholarship Objectives

The scholarship aims to:

  • Increase female participation in STEM teaching careers.
  • Promote equity and inclusion in Uganda’s education sector.
  • Expand the supply of qualified female science teachers.
  • Develop female role models who can inspire the next generation of girls in STEM.

Target Beneficiaries

The program will support:

  • 1,000 female scholars.
  • Young women aged 18–35 years.
  • Refugees and internally displaced persons (7% allocation).
  • Persons with disabilities (5% allocation).
  • Female in-service science, technology, and mathematics teachers seeking to upgrade to a BScEd degree (20% allocation).

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

1. Meet the Target Group Requirements

Applicants must be:

  • Female Ugandan citizens residing in Uganda; or
  • Female refugees living in Uganda; or
  • Female youth with disabilities living in Uganda.

2. Have Admission to an Eligible Institution

Applicants must hold an admission offer for a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) from one of the following institutions:

Universities

  • Makerere University
  • Kyambogo University
  • Busitema University
  • Islamic University in Uganda
  • Gulu University
  • Muni University
  • Mountains of the Moon University
  • Mbarara University of Science and Technology
  • Uganda Martyrs University
  • Kabale University

UNITE Campuses

  • Kabale
  • Kaliro
  • Mubende
  • Muni
  • Unyama

CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO

APPLY HERE

3. Demonstrate Commitment to Teaching

Applicants must show a strong commitment to pursuing a teaching career, particularly in secondary schools, and be willing to serve in rural or underserved communities for at least two years after graduation.

4. Demonstrate Financial Need

Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial need and require support to pursue their university education.

Why Apply for the LIT Uganda STEM Scholarship?

This scholarship goes beyond financial assistance. It provides a pathway for young women to become future STEM educators, leaders, and changemakers who can transform education and inspire girls across Uganda.

By investing in female science teachers, the program contributes to stronger education systems, increased gender equality, and improved learning outcomes for future generations.

How to Apply

Interested and eligible candidates should  prepare the required application documents and submit their applications through the official Leaders in Teaching Uganda Program application platform before the stated deadline.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO

APPLY HERE

Early application is strongly encouraged due to the highly competitive nature of the scholarship.

The LIT Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship represents one of the most significant opportunities currently available for young women seeking fully funded university education in Uganda. If you are passionate about STEM subjects, teaching, and creating lasting impact in your community, this scholarship could be your pathway to a rewarding and meaningful career.

 

CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO

APPLY HERE

The post LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs & Women with Disabilities in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid IOM Internships 2026: International Organization for Migration (IOM) Global Internship Programme – Remote & On-Site Opportunities Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Open | Paid Internships | Rolling Deadlines | Global Locations Are you looking to gain hands-on experience with a United Nations agency while building a career in international development, migration, humanitarian assistance, public policy, or global affairs? The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the UN agency dedicated to migration, is currently recruiting interns for […]

The post Paid IOM Internships 2026: International Organization for Migration (IOM) Global Internship Programme – Remote & On-Site Opportunities Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open | Paid Internships | Rolling Deadlines | Global Locations

Are you looking to gain hands-on experience with a United Nations agency while building a career in international development, migration, humanitarian assistance, public policy, or global affairs?

The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the UN agency dedicated to migration, is currently recruiting interns for multiple paid internship opportunities worldwide. With openings across Europe, Africa, Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East, the IOM Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in a multicultural and international work environment.

Whether you are interested in migration management, humanitarian response, artificial intelligence, health programmes, supply chain operations, human resources, or protection programming, there may be an opportunity that matches your interests and career goals.

About the IOM Internship Programme

The IOM Internship Programme provides talented students and recent graduates with practical exposure to the work of one of the world’s leading humanitarian organizations. Interns contribute to ongoing projects while receiving mentorship and professional development opportunities from experienced international staff.

Benefits of the Programme

Participants may receive:

  • Paid internship opportunities (stipends vary by duty station)
  • Hands-on experience within a United Nations agency
  • Exposure to international migration and humanitarian programming
  • Professional networking opportunities across the UN system
  • Practical skills development in policy, operations, research, and programme management
  • Experience working in diverse and multicultural teams
  • Opportunities to work both remotely and on-site depending on the role

Current IOM Internship Opportunities (2026)

The following internship positions are currently open:

1. Intern – Canada Visa Application Centre (CVAC) – Full Time (Remunerated)

📍 Almaty, Kazakhstan
📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026

2. Intern – Health Assessment Programmes

📍 Athens, Greece
📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026

3. Intern – Protection

📍 Pretoria, South Africa
📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026

4. Intern – Human Resources

📍 Panama City, Panama
📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026

5. Intern – Programme and Liaison Support

📍 Brussels, Belgium
📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026

6. Intern

📍 Bern, Switzerland
📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026

7. Intern – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions and Operational Support

📍 Quito, Ecuador
📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026

8. Intern – Supply Chain

📍 Dakar, Senegal
📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026

9. Intern (CinemArena Project)

📍 Rome, Italy
📅 Deadline: 25 June 2026

10. Internship

📍 New Delhi, India
📅 Deadline: 24 June 2026

Additional internship opportunities may be added regularly through IOM’s recruitment platform. Candidates are encouraged to monitor the vacancies page frequently.

Internship Duration

  • Typical internship length: 2–6 months
  • Extensions may be granted up to 9 months
  • Some duty stations may prefer candidates available for longer placements

Eligibility Criteria

IOM welcomes applications from highly motivated individuals who are passionate about migration, development, humanitarian action, and international cooperation.

Ideal candidates include:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Graduate students
  • Recent graduates
  • Young professionals seeking international experience
  • Candidates from diverse academic disciplines

Relevant fields of study include:

  • International Relations
  • Development Studies
  • Political Science
  • Public Policy
  • Migration Studies
  • Human Rights
  • International Law
  • Public Health
  • Supply Chain Management
  • Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
  • Business Administration
  • Communications and Media Studies

Why Consider an IOM Internship?

Interning with IOM offers much more than work experience. Participants gain first-hand exposure to global migration challenges and solutions while contributing to programmes that impact millions of people worldwide.

Interns often support initiatives related to:

  • Refugee and migrant protection
  • Humanitarian assistance
  • Emergency response
  • Health assessments
  • Labour migration
  • Border management
  • Counter-trafficking efforts
  • Data and research
  • Artificial intelligence and digital transformation
  • Policy development and advocacy

The experience can also serve as a strong pathway toward future careers within the United Nations system, international NGOs, donor agencies, and development organizations.

How to Apply for IOM Internships

Step 1: Create a New IOM Recruitment Account

IOM recently launched a new e-recruitment platform.

If you had an account on the previous recruitment system, you must create a new account before applying for future vacancies.

To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1&selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 

Important: Failure to update your profile may result in automatic disqualification from the recruitment process.

Step 2: Complete Your Candidate Profile

  • Click “Apply Now” on your chosen vacancy
  • Create your candidate profile
  • Upload your CV and supporting documents
  • Complete all required sections carefully

Step 3: Submit Your Application

Review your application thoroughly before submission and ensure all information is accurate and up to date.

Application Tips

To strengthen your application:

  • Highlight relevant academic coursework
  • Include volunteer or professional experience related to migration or humanitarian work
  • Demonstrate cross-cultural experience and international exposure
  • Showcase research, analytical, communication, or project management skills
  • Tailor your application to the specific internship position

If your internship is linked to university requirements or supported by a scholarship programme, mention this clearly in your cover letter.

Beware of Recruitment Scams

IOM has issued an important warning regarding fraudulent recruitment activities.

Remember:

  • IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process.
  • IOM will never request bank account information or passwords.
  • Official communications come only from email addresses ending in @iom.int.

If you receive suspicious messages claiming to represent IOM, report them immediately via:

📧 talentpool@iom.int

Key Details at a Glance

Category Details
Organization International Organization for Migration (IOM)
Programme Global Internship Programme 2026
Funding Paid (stipend varies by location)
Duration 2–9 Months
Locations Worldwide
Eligibility Students & Recent Graduates
Application Deadline Rolling (varies by position)
Work Format On-site and Selected Remote Opportunities

Final Thoughts

The IOM Paid Internship Programme 2026 is one of the most valuable international internship opportunities currently available for students and recent graduates interested in migration, humanitarian action, development, public policy, and global affairs.

With paid placements across multiple continents, opportunities to work alongside international experts, and exposure to real-world humanitarian challenges, the programme provides an excellent platform for launching a career within the United Nations system and the broader international development sector.

Given the competitive nature of IOM internships and the rolling recruitment process, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Apply today and take the next step toward an impactful global career with IOM.

To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1&selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 

Find out more and Start your journey with IOM today. Apply now.

The post Paid IOM Internships 2026: International Organization for Migration (IOM) Global Internship Programme – Remote & On-Site Opportunities Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google Student Researcher Program 2026: Applications Open for Bachelor’s, Master’s and PhD Students Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you looking for a prestigious research internship at one of the world’s leading technology companies? Applications are now open for the Google Student Researcher Program 2026, offering exciting opportunities for Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD students to work alongside Google’s research and engineering teams. The Google Student Researcher Program provides students with the opportunity to […]

The post Google Student Researcher Program 2026: Applications Open for Bachelor’s, Master’s and PhD Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you looking for a prestigious research internship at one of the world’s leading technology companies? Applications are now open for the Google Student Researcher Program 2026, offering exciting opportunities for Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD students to work alongside Google’s research and engineering teams.

The Google Student Researcher Program provides students with the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research projects while gaining hands-on experience in some of the most innovative areas of computer science and technology.

About the Google Student Researcher Program 2026

The Student Researcher Program is designed for students pursuing undergraduate, master’s, and doctoral degrees who are interested in advancing research and innovation in technology. Participants will collaborate with Google researchers and engineers on impactful projects that address real-world challenges.

Available Positions

1. Student Researcher, BS/MS, Winter/Summer 2026

Locations:

  • United States (Mountain View, California; Ann Arbor, Michigan; and multiple additional locations)
  • Canada (Waterloo, Ontario; Montreal, Quebec; and other locations)

Eligibility:

  • Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in:
    • Computer Science
    • Linguistics
    • Statistics
    • Biostatistics
    • Applied Mathematics
    • Operations Research
    • Economics
    • Natural Sciences
    • Related technical fields

2. Student Researcher, PhD, Winter/Summer 2026

Locations:

  • United States (Mountain View, California; Ann Arbor, Michigan; and multiple additional locations)
  • Canada (Waterloo, Ontario; Montreal, Quebec; and other locations)

Eligibility:

  • Currently enrolled in a PhD program in a relevant technical discipline.

3. Student Researcher, 2026 (India)

Location:

  • Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

Eligibility:

  • Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD degree program in relevant technical fields.

Research Areas

Successful applicants may work in one or more of the following areas:

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Machine Learning
  • Deep Learning
  • Natural Language Understanding
  • Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)
  • Computer Vision
  • Data Science
  • Software Engineering
  • Quantum Information Science
  • Algorithmic Foundations of Optimization
  • Related computer science and technology disciplines

Why Apply?

Participants in the Google Student Researcher Program can:

  • Work with world-class researchers and engineers
  • Gain hands-on research experience at Google
  • Contribute to innovative technology projects
  • Build professional networks within the tech industry
  • Strengthen academic and career prospects
  • Develop skills in advanced research and emerging technologies

CLICK HERE TO Visit Google’s official careers portal to review position details, eligibility requirements, available locations, and application instructions.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for students passionate about research, innovation, artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, software engineering, and other advanced computing fields. Students with prior experience in research projects, programming, data analysis, or related technical work are encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

Applications are reviewed according to the requirements and timelines specified for each position. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible.

How to Apply

CLICK HERE TO Visit Google’s official careers portal to review position details, eligibility requirements, available locations, and application instructions.

For more Google related opportunities CLICK HERE 

The post Google Student Researcher Program 2026: Applications Open for Bachelor’s, Master’s and PhD Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) Jobs 2026: Multiple Vacancies Open in South Africa and Rwanda
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
AUDA-NEPAD Recruitment 2026: Apply for Finance, Procurement, Programme and Project Management Jobs Are you looking for an opportunity to work with a leading Pan-African institution committed to advancing Africa’s development agenda? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) has announced several exciting job openings for qualified professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development across the continent. […]

The post African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) Jobs 2026: Multiple Vacancies Open in South Africa and Rwanda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
AUDA-NEPAD Recruitment 2026: Apply for Finance, Procurement, Programme and Project Management Jobs

Are you looking for an opportunity to work with a leading Pan-African institution committed to advancing Africa’s development agenda? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) has announced several exciting job openings for qualified professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development across the continent.

These AUDA-NEPAD vacancies offer the chance to work in key areas such as finance, procurement, water and sanitation, programme implementation, and project management while supporting impactful initiatives that place youth and women at the center of development.

Why Work with AUDA-NEPAD?

The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) is the development agency of the African Union, dedicated to promoting regional integration, economic growth, good governance, and sustainable development across Africa. Working with AUDA-NEPAD provides professionals with the opportunity to make meaningful contributions to Africa’s transformation while collaborating with experts from across the continent.

AUDA-NEPAD Job Vacancies 2026

1. Finance Officer (P2)

  • Deadline: 22 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa
  • Supervisor: Head of Finance

2. Finance Assistant (GSA5)

  • Deadline: 22 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa
  • Supervisor: Finance Officer, Grants

3. Head of Project Delivery Office (P4 Step 5)

  • Deadline: 26 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Kigali, Rwanda
  • Supervisor: Chief of Staff

4. Programme and Implementation Officer (P2 Step 5)

  • Deadline: 26 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Kigali, Rwanda
  • Supervisor: Head of Project Delivery Office

5. Senior Programme Officer, Water and Sanitation (P3)

  • Deadline: 29 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa
  • Supervisor: Project Coordinator – WASH Project

6. Procurement Officer (P2)

  • Deadline: 29 June 2026
  • Duty Station: Centurion, South Africa
  • Supervisor: Senior Procurement Officer

Who Should Apply?

These opportunities are ideal for professionals with backgrounds in:

  • Finance and Accounting
  • Procurement and Supply Chain Management
  • Project Management
  • Programme Implementation
  • Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH)
  • Public Health and Medicines Regulation
  • International Development
  • Public Administration

Candidates with relevant qualifications and experience who are passionate about Africa’s development are encouraged to apply.

How to Apply for AUDA-NEPAD Jobs

Interested applicants can access detailed job descriptions, eligibility requirements, and application instructions through the official AUDA-NEPAD careers portal.

Apply Here: https://www.nepad.org/work-with-us/careers

Applicants experiencing technical challenges during registration or application submission may contact: audahiring@nepad.org

Important Application Deadlines

Position Application Deadline
Finance Officer (P2) 22 June 2026
Finance Assistant (GSA5) 22 June 2026
Head of Project Delivery Office (P4 Step 5) 26 June 2026
Programme and Implementation Officer (P2 Step 5) 26 June 2026
Senior Programme Officer, Water and Sanitation (P3) 29 June 2026
Procurement Officer (P2) 29 June 2026

Apply Here: https://www.nepad.org/work-with-us/careers

If you aspire to work for a respected Pan-African organization driving sustainable development across Africa, these AUDA-NEPAD career opportunities could be the next step in your professional journey. Whether your expertise lies in finance, procurement, programme management, public health, or water and sanitation, these vacancies offer a platform to contribute to Africa’s future while advancing your career.

Apply before the respective deadlines and share these opportunities with colleagues, friends, and professionals who may be interested.

Apply Here: https://www.nepad.org/work-with-us/careers

For more opportunities for Africans, please CLICK HERE
